summary: The reproduction bundle clones the upstream repository at the pinned vulnerable v3.4.1 commit and proves that malformed flatted reference tokens resolve to ambient Array objects and allow reviver-assisted `Array.prototype` pollution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that attacker-controlled tokens resolve to `Array`, `Array.prototype`, and `Array.prototype.map`, and that the documented reviver path can set a proof property on `Array.prototype`.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned the upstream repository at commit `2a02dce7c641dec31194c67663f9b0b12e62da20`, built the Node-based verifier image, and printed the expected proof sequence: `resolved constructor -> Array`, `resolved __proto__ -> Array.prototype`, `resolved map -> Array.prototype.map`, `observed Array.prototype.evohuntPolluted = evohunt-proof`, `confirmed fromJSON([{a:"__proto__"}]).a === Array.prototype`, followed by the final success signal.
verification_method: The verifier loads the pinned JavaScript implementation from the mounted target repo, asserts the ambient-object resolutions, performs reviver-assisted `Array.prototype` pollution, confirms the proof property appears on a new array, and only then prints the success signal.
evidence_produced_by_run: [evohunt] SUCCESS: Array.prototype polluted via attacker-selected reference token

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
